channel.title: Writing on Aman Ahuja
channel.description: Recent content in Writing on Aman Ahuja

item.title: Gathering my writings Part 1: the Pafnuty Blog
item.description:
Although this section of the site is new, there are many posts and papers that I’ve written elsewhere. It’s content sitting on other platforms. I’d like to gather some of that stuff and bring it here.

I posted earlier about why that might be important – but simply put, I’d like to make some disperse content easier to find, to show continuity, to be able to observe my ideas and my writing style evolve over time, and to have ownership over what what I’ve written.

item.title: RSS and POSSE
item.description:
This blog is setup with POSSE in mind – the idea that you can Publish on your Own Site, and then Syndicate Elsewhere. It’s a principle that comes from the IndieWeb community. One can own one’s own content, and keep it under control, even if platforms (like Linkedin, or the Fediverse) are utilized to distribute and share this content.

RSS is also important. RSS is to consuming content what POSSE is to publishing it. Both are about retaining ownership, staying interoperable, keeping switching costs low. Setting up my site with RSS paves a way for those who want to read my content to do so on their own terms.
